diagrams.title: Single-Point Perspective Projection
diagrams.description: A basic vanishing point setup showing a room interior with a single vanishing point on the horizon line. Shows how parallel lines converge to a single point, demonstrating the fundamental principle of perspective projection in computer graphics.
elements: Horizon line (horizontal line across the middle)
Converging horizontal lines (floor tiles, wall edges)
diagrams.purpose: Introduces the core concept of vanishing points and how a single point creates one-point perspective, establishing the foundation for understanding perspective projection in CG rendering

diagrams.title: Two-Point Perspective Projection
diagrams.description: A corner view of a building or room showing two vanishing points on the horizon line. Demonstrates how lines along the X-axis converge to one point and lines along the Z-axis converge to another, while vertical lines remain parallel.
elements: Converging lines to VP1 (shown in one color)
Converging lines to VP2 (shown in different color)
Building/wall surfaces showing the corner perspective
diagrams.purpose: Explains two-point perspective commonly used in architectural visualization and CG, showing how two vanishing points on the horizon create a corner view with orthogonal line convergence

diagrams.title: Vanishing Point Geometry Setup
diagrams.description: A technical diagram showing the geometric relationship between the camera/eye, the projection plane, and vanishing points. Illustrates the mathematical foundation of perspective projection used in computer graphics rendering pipelines.
elements: Camera position / eye point (represented as dot)
Lines from eye through vanishing points
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the geometric principles behind vanishing points in computer graphics, helping understand how perspective transformation matrices work in CG rendering and 3D graphics pipelines
